===================
===================
=== Rolling Thunder Revue 1975-11-04 Providence late.txt
===================
===================
===================
Civic Center
Providence, Rhode Island
4 November 1975  Evening

A different version of this recording was uploaded to DIME a few weeks ago, not without some controversy. That version, reported as a fresh transfer from a first-generation copy supplied by the original taper, presented the show in a nonstandard running order (which didn't match any accounts of the concert, or any other show on the 1975 tour). Several folks familiar with previously circulated versions of the recording have described the new version as, well, not quite so good, and I agree. Therefore:

(contrast clause) While this recording is derived from the same source as the recent torrent of a different transfer, its sound is described as superior by several who have heard both versions. To my ears, the sound is a bit clearer and crisper, and the sound of the individual instruments is better defined. I suspect this torrent was transferred from a lower-gen copy than the more recent version. This torrent also presents the concert in the correct running order.

Whichever version is a more accurate presentation of the concert, there is certainly no disputing that this is, as LK notes in his RTR Guide, one of the finest Dylan audience recordings of the pre-digital era, an extraordinary effort.

Disc 1

1. Good Love Is Hard To Find (Bob Neuwirth)
2. spoken invocation
3. Sleazy (Bob Neuwirth)
4. Cindy (When I Get Home) (Bob Neuwirth)
5. Dont Blame Me (Steven Soles)
6. Werewolves Of London (T-Bone Burnett)
7. Nowhere To Go (Cindy Bullens)
8. Catfish (Rob Stoner)
9. Is There Life On Mars? (Mick Ronson)
10. Alabama Dark (Bob Neuwirth & Ronee Blakley)
11. Please (Ronee Blakley)
12. Mercedes Benz (Bob Neuwirth)
13. Ramblin Jack (Bob Neuwirth)
14. Me And Bobby McGee (Ramblin Jack Elliott)
15. Friend Of The Devil (Ramblin Jack Elliott)
16. Salt Pork, West Virginia (Ramblin Jack Elliott)
17. Im A Rich And Ramblin Boy (Ramblin Jack Elliott)

 
Disc 2	

1. When I Paint My Masterpiece (Dylan/Neuwirth)
2. It Ain't Me, Babe
3. A Hard Rain's A-Gonna Fall
4. Romance In Durango 
5. Isis 
[Intermission]
6. The Times They Are A-Changin' (Dylan/Baez)
7. Never Let Me Go (Dylan/Baez)
8. I Dreamed I Saw St. Augustine (Dylan/Baez)
9. I Shall Be Released (Dylan/Baez)
10. Diamonds And Rust (Joan Baez)
11. Swing Low Sweet Chariot (Joan Baez)
12. Joe Hill (Joan Baez)
13. Love Song To A Stranger (Joan Baez)
14. Long Black Veil (Joan Baez)
15. Please Come To Boston (Joan Baez)

Disc 3

1. Chestnut Mare (Roger McGuinn) [ends at tape flip]
2. [after tape flip] Chestnut Mare (Roger McGuinn)
3. The Night They Drove Old Dixie Down (Joan Baez)
4. It's Alright Ma (I'm Only Bleeding)
5. Oh Sister 
6. Hurricane 
7. One More Cup Of Coffee
8. Sara
9. Just Like A Woman
10. Knockin' On Heaven's Door (Dylan/McGuinn)
11. This Land Is Your Land (Revue)

Two tracks listed here are not listed or discretely tracked at the other torrent. D1T13, Neuwirth's song introducing Jack Elliott, is combined with Elliott's first song. D1T2, the invocation, is included without notation at the end of "Good Love Is Hard To Find."  Acclording to Larry (Ratso) Sloman, the invocation was delivered by a Cherokee "medicine man of some note"  named Rolling Thunder, who Dylan had had flown in from Nevada. I don't believe he appeared at any other RTR performnces.

The recording data for the master tape has been reported as:

taped by Jimmy Warburton from left mid loge
lineage: aud>sony ecm 99a>sony tc-152 cassette master

My guess is that the other torrent became a bit scrambled when the cassette masters were accidentally transferred to reels out of order. This torrent was apparently sourced from a transfer from a different archival copy or from the master cassettes. More information would be welcome.

These flacs come from the 12 DVDR/150 (roughly) CDR Rolling Thunder omnibus, which as I received it did not include detailed lineage information. Seeding help welcomed, particulary since my cable ISP tends to choke during Sunday night football.
===================
===================
===================
